channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hope and dread as Israelis and Palestinians await a Gaza truce that may not end their suffering
item.description: Officials now say they are closer than ever to reaching an agreement to pause the Israel-Hamas war and release hostages held in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Lidman, Sam Mednick And Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Live updates: Rubio, key Trump nominees appear for confirmation hearings
item.description: Half a dozen nominees to serve in President-elect Trump's administration are set to appear before Senate committees for confirmation hearings on Wednesday. The list includes former Florida Attorney General Pam Bondi, Trump's nominee for attorney general; Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian prime minister says Palestinian Authority should run Gaza in future
item.description: The Palestinian prime minister says it's unacceptable for any entity other than the Palestinian Authority to run the Gaza Strip in the future, as Israel and Hamas appear to be at the closest point yet to sealing a deal to end 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Africa tried to pressure informal gold miners by cutting off food. Now it's pulling out bodies
item.description: Rescuers and volunteers have pulled at least 78 dead miners and more than 160 survivors from an abandoned South African gold mine.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Impeached South Korean president detained weeks after martial law chaos
item.description: South Korean law enforcement detained impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol to bring him in for questioning following his short-lived martial law declaration last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel supplied Iran with centrifuge platforms containing explosives, top official acknowledges
item.description: Israel supplied Iran with centrifuge platforms containing explosives for its nuclear enrichment program in an act of sabotage.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Polish leader vows to use EU presidency to speed up Ukraine’s membership quest
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk vowed on Wednesday to use his country’s presidency of the European Union to push forward with Ukraine’s membership quest as the two sides reported progress in recent days on a major irritant in their relations.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US foes Iran, Russia, other UN diplomats exempt from NYC congestion pricing as taxpayers forced to foot bill
item.description: The U.S. State Department has exempted U.N. diplomats and missions in New York City from paying new congestion fees according to recent guidance published by the government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India's navy launches submarine, warships to guard against China's presence in Indian Ocean
item.description: MUMBAI, India (AP) — India's navy on Wednesday simultaneously launched a submarine, a destroyer and a frigate built at a state-run shipyard, underscoring the importance of protecting the Indian Ocean region through which 95% of the country's trade moves amid a strong Chinese presence.
item.dc:creator: RAFIQ MAQBOOL and ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bangladesh Supreme Court acquits ex-Prime Minister Zia, clearing the way for her to run in elections
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s Supreme Court on Wednesday acquitted former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia in the last corruption case against her, paving the way for her to run in elections that an interim government says will be held either in December or in the first half of 2026.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the detention of impeached South Korean President Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — After weeks of being holed up in a heavily guarded compound, impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has been detained by hundreds of police and anti-corruption officials for questioning over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 5 killed in Mexican town caught between battling criminal groups
item.description: HUITZILAC, Mexico (AP) — Schools and some businesses were closed and few people walked the streets of this town south of Mexico’s capital Tuesday, hours after five people were gunned down on the same street where another attack left eight dead just eight months earlier.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president Yoon Suk Yeol arrested: report
item.description: Disgraced South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was reportedly arrested over insurrection charges stemming from last month's shocking martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has been taken to a detention center near Seoul over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Dozens of survivors and dead pulled from abandoned South African mine as hundreds remain underground
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa (AP) — Months after South African authorities initially cut off supplies to miners working illegally in an abandoned gold mine, rescuers brought dozens of bodies and emaciated survivors to the surface Tuesday with hundreds more still believed to be underground, many of them
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: 84-year-old man fights off robber using pair of jeans
item.description: YORKSHIRE, U.K. (WFLA) — An 84-year-old man used a pair of jeans to fight off a would-be robber as he did his laundry in South Yorkshire.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: France’s new prime minister says he'll renegotiate a contested plan to raise the retirement age
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s new prime minister François Bayrou announced Tuesday the renegotiation of a contested plan raising the retirement age from 62 to 64, in a crucial move to seek more stability for his minority government.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy, Macron discuss Western troop deployment in Ukraine before German defense chief visits
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that he has held further discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron about the possibility of Western troops deploying in Ukraine to safeguard any peace deal ending the nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada prepared to buy more from US amid Trump tariff threats: Ambassador
item.description: Canada’s Ambassador to the U.S. affirmed that the country is willing to boost American imports in the wake of President-elect Trump’s threats to increase tariffs. “We are happy to source what we can from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Irregular migration into the European Union fell sharply last year, border agency says
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The number of irregular border crossings into the European Union fell significantly in 2024, according to the bloc's border control agency Frontex, something which it attributed to intensified cooperation against smuggling networks.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kate returns to cancer center where she was treated to offer gratitude to medical teams
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales visited the hospital where she was treated for cancer on Tuesday, offering gratitude to the medical teams who helped her and give support to patients now undergoing treatment.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill 18 in Gaza, medics say, as Hamas accepts a ceasefire draft
item.description: Israeli strikes in the Gaza Strip killed at least 18 people overnight including women and children, health officials said Tuesday, as Israel and Hamas appeared to be narrowing in on a ceasefire deal to end the 15-month war and release dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO announces a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO is launching a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region after a string of incidents that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the strategic region, the alliance's leader said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top aide of impeached South Korean president pleads for investigators to halt detention efforts
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The top aide of impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol pleaded with law enforcement agencies on Tuesday to abandon their efforts to detain him over his imposition of martial law last month, as authorities prepared a second attempt to take him into custody.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Ugandan military court rules it can try opposition figure on treachery charge carrying death penalty
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — A prominent opposition figure in Uganda will stand trial on the serious charge of treachery, a military court ruled Tuesday, escalating the legal trouble Kizza Besigye faces ahead of presidential elections scheduled for 2026.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: White House holds press briefing as latest Israel-Hamas ceasefire emerges
item.description: The White House held a press briefing on Monday in the midst of another ceasefire deal emerging between Israel and Hamas as the war in Gaza rages on. Several sticking points remain as officials work to secure an end to more than one year of fighting in the coastal enclave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden calls for immediate cease-fire in call with Israel's Netanyahu
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a phone call on Sunday to discuss the ongoing ceasefire and hostage release negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine has captured 2 North Korean soldiers, South Korea's intelligence service says
item.description: Ukraine captured two wounded North Korean soldiers who were fighting on behalf of Russia in a Russian border region, South Korea’s intelligence service said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black boxes from South Korea plane crash failed to record final 4 minutes, officials say
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The black boxes of a Boeing jetliner that crashed in South Korea last month stopped recording about four minutes before the accident, South Korean officials said Saturday, possibly complicating investigations into the cause of the disaster that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, NewsNation
